_about: Hand-labeled ground truth for the CLEAN evaluation set: 88 cropped, label-only retail bottle/can photos (no duplicates, no junk), each labeled by viewing it directly. This set replaces the older mixed-quality batches as the primary eval. Labels capture what is objectively true in each photo: the facts a human reviewer agrees on regardless of how the AI reads them. Images are not in git — download from https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1hCq_woq7IwyrOwbi_9XLHeSUsduSUxjN?usp=sharing into public/samples/alcohol/. The harness (run.mjs) scores the extractor against these; last run output: eval/results.json.

_fields.isAlcohol: Is this an alcoholic beverage at all?
_fields.beverageType: spirits | wine | beer | other — the true product class
_fields.brand: The true brand as a human reads it (null if not visible in frame)
_fields.abvVisible: Is an alcohol-content statement physically legible in THIS photo?
_fields.warningPresent: Is a government warning physically present & legible enough to read in THIS photo?
_fields.warningIsFederal: If present, is it the federal 'GOVERNMENT WARNING:' statement (true) vs state/other (false) vs n/a (null)?
_fields.photoDifficulty: easy | moderate | hard — human judgment of legibility
